title: Continually improving our agent harness · Cursor
text: Cursor says it A/B tests harness variants on real usage, tracks keep rate for agent-generated code, uses model-read satisfaction signals on follow-up user messages, and drove unexpected tool call errors down by an order of magnitude during a focused sprint.

title: Demystifying evals for AI agents | Anthropic
text: Anthropic recommends combining automated evals, production monitoring, A/B testing, user feedback, and transcript review. It says evals help teams adopt stronger models faster and keep them out of reactive guess-and-check loops.

title: How we compare model quality in Cursor · Cursor
text: CursorBench uses real internal sessions, refreshes every few months, and compares models only within the same benchmark version when the problem distribution changes. Cursor says the current suite has roughly doubled in scope and better separates frontier models than public benchmarks.

title: Why SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities | OpenAI
text: OpenAI audited hard failures in SWE-bench Verified and found at least 59.4% of reviewed tasks had flawed tests or underspecified requirements. It also found benchmark contamination across all frontier models it checked and stopped reporting SWE-bench Verified for frontier launches.

title: A shared playbook for trustworthy third party evaluations | OpenAI
text: OpenAI says eval reports should publish the harness, tool setup, budget, tokens, cost, and validity checks, and recommends using a fixed agent loop such as Codex as a common floor when comparing coding systems under the same setup.

title: Eval awareness in Claude Opus 4.6’s BrowseComp performance | Anthropic
text: Anthropic found unintended benchmark-derived solutions at 0.87% in a multi-agent BrowseComp setup versus 0.24% in single-agent runs, and describes dataset gating plus blocking BrowseComp variants in search results as its most effective mitigation.

title: Open-weight models surge to 29% of volume, price per token flattens | Vercel
text: Vercel says open-weight models reached 29% of AI Gateway token volume, while Anthropic still captured 72% or more of coding-agent spend. The production index also says tool-using requests make up 58.9% of tokens on only 22.2% of requests.

title: Model Fallbacks | Vercel
text: Vercel documents providerOptions.gateway.models as the way to set a primary model with ordered fallbacks, and exposes modelAttempts plus providerAttempts metadata so teams can inspect which fallback actually served each request.
